title: New Ebola Outbreak Hits Congo and Uganda
subtitle: Health officials work to stop the spread of a rare and dangerous virus strain

summary.en: A new Ebola outbreak has caused 65 deaths in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The virus has now spread across the border into Uganda. Health experts are worried because this outbreak involves a rare strain of the virus. Current vaccines and treatments were designed for a different, more common strain. International groups are meeting to coordinate a response to this health emergency.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國爆發新一波伊波拉疫情，已造成六十五人死亡，且病毒已跨越國界傳播至烏干達。衛生專家對此感到憂心，因為此次疫情涉及一種罕見的病毒株，而現有的疫苗與治療方法皆是針對另一種較常見的病毒株所設計。國際組織正召開會議，以協調應對這場健康緊急事件。

paragraphs.en: A serious health crisis is unfolding in Central Africa. According to The Guardian World, an outbreak of Ebola has led to 65 deaths in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). The disease is a type of haemorrhagic fever, which is a severe illness that causes internal bleeding and has a very high death rate. So far, there have been 246 suspected cases reported, mostly in the Ituri province. This area is particularly difficult to manage because it is home to many mining towns where people move in and out constantly.
paragraphs.zh: 中非地區正爆發一場嚴重的健康危機。根據《衛報》國際版（The Guardian World）報導，剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of the Congo, DRC）爆發伊波拉疫情，已造成六十五人死亡。這種疾病屬於出血熱的一種，是一種會導致體內出血且致死率極高的嚴重疾病。目前已接獲兩百四十六起疑似病例，多數集中在伊圖里省（Ituri）。由於該地區有許多礦業城鎮，人員流動頻繁，使得疫情管理格外困難。

paragraphs.en: The situation has become even more concerning as the virus has crossed international borders. Ars Technica reports that officials in Uganda have confirmed a case in their capital city, Kampala. The patient was a 59-year-old man who had recently traveled from the DRC. Because of the high number of people traveling between these countries, health leaders are worried that the disease could spread quickly to other regions.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著病毒跨越國界，情況變得更加令人擔憂。《科技藝術》（Ars Technica）報導，烏干達官員已證實其首都坎帕拉出現確診病例。該名患者為一名五十九歲男子，近期曾從剛果民主共和國入境。由於這些國家之間的人員往來頻繁，衛生官員擔心疾病可能會迅速蔓延至其他地區。

paragraphs.en: This is not the first time the DRC has faced this virus. The country has seen 16 previous outbreaks since Ebola was first discovered there in 1976. However, this current event is different and more complicated than past experiences. Most previous outbreaks in the region were caused by the Zaire strain of the virus. For the Zaire strain, medical experts have already developed effective vaccines—substances that help the body fight off a disease—and treatments. According to The Guardian World, laboratory tests show that this current outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain. There is currently no licensed vaccine for this specific version of the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 這並非剛果民主共和國首次面臨此病毒。自一九七六年伊波拉病毒首次在該國被發現以來，已發生過十六次疫情。然而，此次事件與過去不同，情況也更為複雜。該地區過去多數疫情是由薩伊（Zaire）病毒株所引起，針對此病毒株，醫學專家已研發出有效的疫苗（即能協助人體對抗疾病的物質）與治療方法。根據《衛報》國際版報導，實驗室檢測顯示，此次疫情是由邦迪布焦（Bundibugyo）病毒株所引起，目前針對此特定病毒版本尚無核准的疫苗。

paragraphs.en: Jean-Jacques Muyembe, a leading Congolese expert who helped discover the Ebola virus, explained to the media that this change in strain makes the situation much harder to control. Because the existing medical tools were built to fight the Zaire strain, they may not work well against this one. This creates a difficult challenge for doctors and scientists who are trying to save lives.
paragraphs.zh: 曾協助發現伊波拉病毒的剛果首席專家穆耶姆貝（Jean-Jacques Muyembe）向媒體解釋，病毒株的改變使得疫情更難控制。由於現有的醫療工具是針對薩伊病毒株所設計，對於此病毒株可能效果不彰，這對致力於拯救生命的醫師與科學家而言是一項艱鉅的挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Experts point to several reasons why these outbreaks happen so often in this part of the world. Dr. Michael Head, a researcher from the University of Southampton, noted that a "perfect storm" of factors is likely responsible. These include the tropical climate, large areas of rainforest, and the fact that humans often come into close contact with animals like bats or primates that carry the virus. When these factors combine with the movement of people between rural villages and busy urban centers, it becomes very easy for the virus to jump from animals to humans and then spread through communities.
paragraphs.zh: 專家指出該地區疫情頻發的原因有多種。南安普敦大學（University of Southampton）的研究員海德博士（Dr. Michael Head）指出，這很可能是多種因素共同造成的「完美風暴」。這些因素包括熱帶氣候、廣大的雨林區域，以及人類經常與攜帶病毒的蝙蝠或靈長類動物密切接觸。當這些因素結合鄉村與繁忙城市中心之間的人員流動時，病毒便極易從動物傳播給人類，進而在社區中擴散。

paragraphs.en: Ebola spreads through direct contact with the body fluids of someone who is sick, such as blood or vomit. It can also spread during traditional funeral practices if people touch the body of someone who has died from the disease. This makes community education a vital part of any response effort.
paragraphs.zh: 伊波拉病毒是透過直接接觸病患的體液（如血液或嘔吐物）進行傳播。在傳統喪葬儀式中，若人們觸碰死於該疾病者的遺體，也可能導致感染。因此，社區衛教成為任何防疫工作中至關重要的一環。

paragraphs.en: In response to the growing danger, the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) is taking urgent action. They are organizing a meeting with leaders from the DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan, as well as the World Health Organization and major pharmaceutical companies. Dr. Jean Kaseya, the director general of Africa CDC, stated that regional coordination is essential to stop the virus from moving further. He emphasized that his organization stands with the people of the DRC during this difficult time.
paragraphs.zh: 為應對日益嚴峻的威脅，非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention, Africa CDC）正採取緊急行動。他們正籌備一場會議，邀請剛果民主共和國、烏干達與南蘇丹的領導人，以及世界衛生組織（World Health Organization）與各大製藥公司共同參與。非洲疾管中心主任卡塞亞博士（Dr. Jean Kaseya）表示，區域協調對於阻止病毒進一步擴散至關重要。他強調，該組織在這一艱難時刻將與剛果民主共和國人民站在一起。

paragraphs.en: As the world watches, the priority remains containing the virus within the affected zones. With the memory of the massive 2014-2016 outbreak in West Africa—which resulted in thousands of deaths—still fresh in the minds of health officials, the goal is to act as quickly as possible. For now, the focus is on tracking the virus, treating the sick, and preventing further contact that could lead to more infections.
paragraphs.zh: 全球正密切關注此事，當前的首要任務仍是將病毒控制在受影響區域內。由於二○一四至二○一六年西非那場造成數千人死亡的大規模疫情，至今仍讓衛生官員記憶猶新，因此目標是盡可能採取最迅速的行動。目前的工作重點在於追蹤病毒、治療病患，並防止可能導致更多感染的進一步接觸。

keyTerms.noteZh: 通常指摺疊物展開，這裡指事件的發展。文中：A serious health crisis is unfolding in Central Africa.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where several bad events or conditions happen at the same time, making the overall result much worse.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 完美風暴；指多種不利因素同時發生，導致災難性後果。
keyTerms.noteZh: 並非指真正的天氣現象，而是比喻多重因素疊加。文中：Dr. Michael Head, a researcher from the University of Southampton, noted that a "perfect storm" of factors is likely responsible.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To support or show solidarity with someone during a difficult time.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡不是指物理上的站立，而是表達支持。文中：He emphasized that his organization stands with the people of the DRC during this difficult time.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Recently remembered or still very clear in one's mind.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 記憶猶新的；印象深刻的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指食物新鮮，這裡指記憶清晰。文中：With the memory of the massive 2014-2016 outbreak in West Africa—which resulted in thousands of deaths—still fresh in the minds of health officials, the goal is to act as quickly as possible.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths

quiz.question: What is the primary reason why the current Ebola outbreak in the DRC is considered more difficult to manage than previous ones?

options.A: The death rate of the current strain is significantly higher than the Zaire strain.
options.B: The current outbreak is caused by a strain for which there is no licensed vaccine.
options.C: The virus has already spread to more countries than in any of the 16 previous outbreaks.
options.D: The healthcare infrastructure in the Ituri province has collapsed due to mining activities.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，過去的疫情多由 Zaire 病毒株引起，已有對應疫苗；但此次疫情經實驗室檢測為 Bundibugyo 病毒株，目前尚無核准的疫苗。選項 A 文中未提及死亡率比較；選項 C 錯誤，文中提到有 16 次疫情，並未說這次擴散國家最多；選項 D 雖然提到 Ituri 省難以管理，但並非此次疫情比以往「更難控制」的主要原因。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why is the Ituri province specifically identified as a high-risk area for disease transmission?

options.A: It is the region where the Ebola virus was originally discovered in 1976.
options.B: It lacks the tropical climate and rainforests necessary to contain the virus.
options.C: Its population is highly mobile due to the presence of numerous mining towns.
options.D: It is the only area in the DRC that refuses to cooperate with the Africa CDC.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第一段，Ituri 省因為有許多採礦城鎮，人員流動頻繁，導致疫情難以控制。選項 A 錯誤，文中未指明 Ituri 是發現地；選項 B 與第五段矛盾，該地區具備熱帶氣候與雨林；選項 D 文中並未提到該地區拒絕合作，反而強調了跨區域協調的重要性。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the author mean by the phrase a "perfect storm" of factors?

options.A: A series of extreme weather events that destroyed medical facilities.
options.B: A combination of unfortunate circumstances that create a severe situation.
options.C: A sudden change in the virus that makes it resistant to all known treatments.
options.D: A coordinated effort by international organizations to fight the outbreak.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。「Perfect storm」在此處為慣用語，意指多種不利因素同時發生，導致情況變得極度嚴重。文中用此來形容熱帶氣候、雨林環境與人類活動接觸頻繁的加乘效應。選項 A 誤解了「storm」的字面意思；選項 C 描述的是病毒變異，而非該詞組的定義；選項 D 則與該詞組所表達的負面情境完全相反。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: The Ebola virus has become a permanent feature of life in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
options.B: International pharmaceutical companies are failing to provide enough resources to the DRC.
options.C: The DRC is facing a challenging Ebola outbreak that requires urgent, coordinated regional action.
options.D: Traditional funeral practices are the primary cause of the current Ebola crisis in Central Africa.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體架構從疫情現況、病毒株變異的困難度、地理與人為因素，到最後提到 Africa CDC 的緊急會議與跨國協調，旨在說明這次疫情的複雜性與急迫性。選項 A 過於悲觀且非文章重點；選項 B 文中未批評藥廠；選項 D 僅是傳播途徑之一，並非文章的核心主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
